revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八大竜王]][[simple:Eight Great Dragon Kings]][[zh:八大龍王]]
{{Refimprove|date=October 3, 2015|qq=}} [[File:Hachi Dairyuuoo Nagaraja.jpg|thumb|150px|Nanda Dragon King, one of the Eight Great Dragon Kings]] The '''Eight Great Dragon Kings''' (八大龍王, Hachidai Ryūō) are eight dragon kings belonging to the {{ill|Eight Legions|en|Eight Legions|qid=Q1045328|lt=Eight Legions|1=Eight Legions}}, featured in the {{ill|Lotus Sutra|en|Lotus Sutra|qid=Q861212|lt=Lotus Sutra|1=Lotus Sutra}}. They serve as protectors of Buddhist law. In the Lotus Sutra’s introductory chapter, the Eight Great Dragon Kings, along with countless other dragon subordinates, are described as having gathered on {{ill|Vulture Peak|en|Vulture Peak|qid=Q19755572|lt=Mount Grdhrakuta|1=Vulture Peak}} to listen to Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha’s teachings, alongside {{ill|Sixteen Arhats|en|Sixteen Arhats|qid=Q3477968|lt=Sixteen Arhats|1=Sixteen Arhats}}, celestial beings, and various bodhisattvas. In Mahayana Buddhism, it is taught that Shakyamuni Tathāgata expounded on the deeds of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}} in Chapter 25 of the Lotus Sutra, "The Universal Gateway of Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva." Through this, the dragon kings attained the supreme {{ill|enlightenment in Buddhism|ja|阿耨多羅三藐三菩提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anuttara Samyak Sambodhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正覚|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1137019|lt=enlightenment|1=Enlightenment in Buddhism}} and became dharma-protecting deities. In ancient India, they were known as '''Nāga''' (नाग), depicted as semi-human, semi-serpentine beings. Over time, as Buddhism spread to China and Japan, the Nāga were transformed into the dragons familiar in East Asian culture. == List == The following eight dragon kings are generally listed in this order: # {{ill|Shesha|en|Shesha|qid=Q2418874|lt=Nanda|1=Shesha}} (難陀竜王) (नन्द, Ānanda) #: Translation: "Joy." Nanda and Upananda are brother dragon kings who once fought against Sāgara. They are mentioned in the 16th chapter of the {{ill|Amoghapasha Sutra|ja|不空羂索神変真言経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amoghapasha Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # {{ill|Upananda|en|跋難陀|ja|跋難陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upananda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (उपनन्द, Upananda) #: Translation: "Minor Joy." Nanda's younger brother, who protected the {{ill|Magadha|en|Magadha|qid=Q234009|lt=Magadha|1=Magadha}} region and participated in Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha's teachings. # {{ill|Sāgara|en|娑伽羅|ja|娑伽羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sāgara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (सागर, Sāgara) #: Translation: "Ocean." The dragon king of the sea palace. He is the father of the eight-year-old dragon girl who appears in the Devadatta chapter of the Lotus Sutra. # {{ill|Vasuki|en|Vasuki|qid=Q1635469|lt=Vasuki|1=Vasuki}} (वासुकि, Vāsuki) #: Known as the "Nine-Headed Dragon King" in Japan, symbolizing vast power and protection. # {{ill|Takshaka|en|Takshaka|qid=Q2633766|lt=Takshaka|1=Takshaka}} (तक्षक, Takṣaka) #: Translation: "Multi-Tongue." This dragon king is said to have venomous powers capable of killing with a glare. # {{ill|Anavatapta|en|阿那婆達多|ja|アナヴァタプタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (अनवतप्त, Anavatapta) #: Translation: "Cool and Untroubled." The guardian of the mythological lake {{ill|Anavatapta Lake|ja|阿耨達池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anavatapta Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Himalayas. # {{ill|Manasvin|en|摩那斯|ja|マナスヴィン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manasvin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (मनस्विन, Manasvin) #: Translation: "Great Body." He is noted for his role in pushing back Asura's seawater attacks on Mount Sumeru. # {{ill|Utpala|en|優鉢羅|ja|ウッパラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utpala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (उत्पल, Utpalaka) #: Translation: "Blue Lotus." The dragon king associated with producing blue lotuses. <gallery heights="250" widths="240"> File:Asakusa sensoji06s3200.jpg|Sāgara Dragon King ({{ill|Sensō-ji|en|Sensō-ji|qid=Q615183|lt=Sensō-ji|1=Sensō-ji}}) File:HachidaiRyuou01.JPG|Eight Great Dragon Kings Shrine, {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|qid=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}} File:三澤寺 八大龍王像.jpg|Dragon King statues at {{ill|Misawa Temple|ja|三澤寺 (伊那市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misawa Temple|qq=}} </gallery> == Shrines and Temples Dedicated to the Eight Great Dragon Kings == [[File:Hachidairyuou-naganouchi.jpg|thumb|Naganochi Eight Great Dragon Kings Shrine]] [[File:Hachidairyuou-mougi.jpg|thumb|Mougi Eight Great Dragon Kings Shrine]] The Eight Great Dragon Kings are worshiped across Japan as deities of rain. Various shrines and small temples are dedicated to them: * {{ill|Amanoiwato Shrine|en|Amanoiwato Shrine|qid=Q2841102|lt=Ama-no-Iwato Shrine|1=Amanoiwato Shrine}} vicinity, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|qid=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}} in the Kansai region, notable for its connection to the {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Shuku|en|Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qid=Q11620888|lt=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku}}. * {{ill|Ryusenji Temple|ja|龍泉寺 (奈良県天川村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryusenji Temple|1=Ryūsen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11679276}}, {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|en|Tenkawa, Nara|qid=Q1203501|lt=Tenkawa|1=Tenkawa, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kongosho Temple|en|金剛證寺|ja|金剛証寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongosho Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金剛證寺|1=Kongōshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3198670}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. == Literature == A poem by {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|en|Minamoto no Sanetomo|qid=Q174249|lt=Minamoto no Sanetomo|1=Minamoto no Sanetomo}} in the {{ill|Kin'yō Wakashū|ja|金槐和歌集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin'yō Wakashū|1=Kinkai Wakashū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11647307}} reads: {{Quotation|時により過ぐれば民の嘆きなり 八大龍王雨やめたまへ|qq=}} Translation: "Excessive rain causes people sorrow; O Eight Great Dragon Kings, please cease the rain."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en|仏の一覧|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nāga|en|ナーガ|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nāga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en|密教|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esoteric Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Buddhism2|qq=}}

[[ja:八大龍王]]
